minimum_image_evidence: The claimed object and relevant part should be visible clearly enough to inspect the claimed condition.
Each submitted image should be considered separately; at least one relevant image should show the claimed object or part clearly enough to evaluate the claim.
The claimed car panel or bumper should be visible from an angle where surface marks or deformation can be assessed.
The claimed glass, light, mirror, or component should be visible clearly enough to inspect cracks, breakage, or missing parts.
When the claim depends on vehicle identity, side, or orientation, the image set should show enough context to match the claimed vehicle and part.
The claimed laptop screen, keyboard, or trackpad area should be visible clearly enough to inspect cracks, stains, missing keys, or surface damage.

applies_to: hinge, lid, corner, body, or port
minimum_image_evidence: The claimed laptop hinge, lid, corner, body, base, or port should be visible with enough context to identify the relevant laptop part.
The package exterior and claimed side, corner, flap, or seal should be visible clearly enough to inspect packaging damage.
The affected package surface or label should be visible clearly enough to assess stain, water damage, label readability, or label damage.
The opened package and relevant contents area should be visible clearly enough to assess missing or damaged items.
The submitted images should provide visual evidence that is usable, relevant to the claim, and grounded in the claimed object.
